title: Thelypteris demerarana
taxonomy: Thelypteris demerarana is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, commonly known as the marsh fern or the marsh leather fern. It belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen ferns. The genus Thelypteris is further classified under the order Polypodiales, which includes various types of ferns. At the kingdom level, Thelypteris demerarana falls under Plantae, as it is a vascular plant. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species within the plant kingdom.
uses: Thelypteris demerarana has been found to have potential medicinal uses in traditional medicine. In some regions, its extracts are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin irritations. intro: Thelypteris demerarana, also known as the Guianan shield fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and oxidative stress, due to its purported health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and protecting against certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris demerarana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thelypteris demerarana is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often consumed to promote overall well-being and to alleviate various health conditions.
